text.title: Spring Boot Transaction, Fetch, Mapping, and Agent Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for transaction placement, fetch strategy, DTO mapping, and common LLM mistakes to reject.

rule.statement: Place @Transactional only on public, non-final service methods. Never on controllers, private methods, or final methods.
rule.rationale: Spring proxies only intercept public non-final methods. Placing @Transactional on a controller or private method silently does nothing.

rule.statement: Never call a @Transactional method from within the same bean (self-invocation). The proxy is bypassed and the transaction does not start.
rule.rationale: Spring AOP proxies intercept calls from outside the bean only. Self-calls go direct, bypassing the proxy.

rule.statement: Always use FetchType.LAZY on associations. Use JOIN FETCH in @Query or @EntityGraph when the association is needed. Never use FetchType.EAGER.
rule.rationale: EAGER loads the association even when unused, causing N+1 on collections. LAZY + explicit fetch is always controllable.

rule.statement: Use @JsonIgnore on all lazy collection fields exposed directly (e.g., bidirectional @OneToMany). Prefer DTO projection to avoid infinite recursion.
rule.rationale: Jackson serializes lazy proxies, triggering the query; bidirectional relations cause infinite recursion without @JsonIgnore.

rule.statement: Never return JPA entities from controllers or service public methods. Always map to a response DTO at the service layer.
rule.rationale: Returning entities exposes internal schema, lazy-load exceptions, and prevents adding computed fields.

rule.statement: Use MapStruct for entity-DTO mapping. Pin lombok + mapstruct-processor + lombok-mapstruct-binding in the maven-compiler-plugin annotationProcessorPaths in that order.
rule.rationale: Without the binding artifact, Lombok generates setters after MapStruct reads fields, producing null mappers at runtime.

rule.statement: @Async methods must return CompletableFuture<T> or void. Use CompletableFuture<T> when the caller needs the result. Use void only for fire-and-forget with caught exceptions inside.
rule.rationale: Async @Async void methods swallow exceptions silently. CompletableFuture propagates them to the caller.

rule.statement: Never call @Async methods from within the same bean. The proxy is bypassed and the call runs synchronously.
rule.rationale: Same proxy bypass as @Transactional — Spring AOP does not intercept self-calls.

rule.statement: Declare a named ThreadPoolTaskExecutor bean per workload type (e.g., emailExecutor, reportExecutor). Never share the default executor across unrelated tasks.
rule.rationale: Sharing the default executor causes head-of-line blocking when slow tasks (reports) block fast ones (emails).

rule.statement: Reject diffs that import javax.* in a Boot 3.x project. Boot 3 requires jakarta.* namespaces. Reject WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter — use SecurityFilterChain bean instead.
rule.rationale: Boot 3 / Spring 6 migrated to Jakarta EE. WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter was removed in Spring Security 6.

rule.statement: Reject @Autowired field injection. Require constructor injection via @RequiredArgsConstructor (Lombok) or explicit constructor.
rule.rationale: Field injection hides required dependencies, breaks immutability, and makes unit testing harder.

rule.statement: Reject service methods that call findAll() without Pageable. Every list endpoint must accept Pageable.
rule.rationale: findAll() without pagination loads the entire table into memory, causing OOM in production.

text.reference: CI gate that enforces these rules mechanically
